howItWorks.intro: Calculate the total volume of soil mix needed based on your raised bed dimensions. Convert to bags or bulk delivery amounts.
rules: Rectangular: volume = length × width × depth (in feet)
Convert inches to feet: divide depth by 12
Soil mix weighs approximately 40 lbs per cubic foot
howItWorks.outro: For beds deeper than 18 inches, you can fill the bottom third with logs, branches, or straw (hugelkultur method) to save on soil cost and improve drainage.

faq.question: How deep should a raised bed be?
faq.answer: Minimum 6 inches for lettuce and herbs, 12 inches for most vegetables, 18-24 inches for root crops like carrots and potatoes. If placed on hard ground or concrete, go deeper since roots can't penetrate below.

faq.question: What soil mix should I use?
faq.answer: A popular mix is 1/3 topsoil, 1/3 compost, and 1/3 aeration (perlite, vermiculite, or coarse sand). Mel's Mix from Square Foot Gardening uses 1/3 peat moss, 1/3 vermiculite, 1/3 blended compost.

faq.question: Is bulk delivery cheaper than bags?
faq.answer: Yes, usually by 50-70%. Bulk soil costs $30-60 per cubic yard delivered, while bagged soil at $5-8 per 2 cu ft bag costs $67-108 per cubic yard equivalent. Bulk makes sense for anything over 1 cubic yard.

faq.question: How much does the soil settle?
faq.answer: New soil settles 10-20% in the first season. Plan to top off your beds annually with 1-2 inches of compost, which also replenishes nutrients.
